course_description:
This course applies marketing concepts and theories to tourism and events contexts and organisations. Marketing theories, models, methods as practices are critically evaluated and applied in real world tourism and events settings.

Tourism and events represent unique forms of service product development to satisfy diverse stakeholders including consumers (residents and/or tourists), government, community and cultural groups, media and business sponsors or financiers. As such, the importance of developing an effective marketing strategy is increasingly important for destinations as well as tourism, travel, and event organisations. Long term and short term marketing strategies for tourism and events organisations are essential in the dynamic and competitive global environment. The post-pandemic recovery and development of the tourism sector, together with a high level of growth in business, sports, and arts events, point to the need for enhanced marketing and management skills. This course outlines the principles required for the successful marketing of tourism and events experiences. Course content includes the principles of segmentation, targeting and positioning, branding and brand management, marketing mix strategies, and marketing in the digital era, all contextualised to pertain to the specific fields of tourism and events.

Course Changes in Response to Previous Student Feedback

A lecture has been provided that focuses on virtual influencer marketer and artificial intelligence to represent what is used within the tourism industry. This focus on virtual influencer marketing and marketing intelligence has also been added to the marketing plan.

course_aims: This undergraduate course aims to introduce students to the marketing of tourism and events. Specifically, it aims to teach students how to develop effective marketing strategies that will enable tourism, travel and event organisations to survive in the dynamic and competitive global environment. It also aims to teach students how to design infographics and gain the necessary skills to conduct a market analysis that can be applied to an events and tourism marketing setting.

learning_outcomes.description: Understand the scope of marketing and its role in tourism and events.
Discuss the different marketing theories and concepts as they apply to the study of tourism and events marketing.
Analyse markets, market demand and experiences from the visitor/resident perspectives
Evaluate the marketing practices of a tourism or events destination or organisation.
As a group, design a marketing plan and develop marketing mix strategies to be used by a tourism or events organisation

assessment_details.title: Market Analysis
assessment_details.task_description: TRVT2001: Tourism & Event Marketing Type: Market Analysis Assessment Percentage: 35% Word Limit : 3,500 words Learning Objectives Assessed: 1, 2, 3, 4 (Refer to Course ECP) Due Date : Wednesday 1st of April, 2026 at 1pm Assignment Type: Individual THE SCENARIO Tangalooma Island Resort ( www.tangalooma.com ) is a tourist resort located on Moreton Island, which is approximately 40km off the coast of Queensland, near Brisbane. After a successful 2025, TIR seeks to further their reputation as a leading eco-certified tourism organisation. They are seeking advice in designing a new marketing plan focusing on eco-certified tourism experiences for their activities and events planned for 2026. This marketing and communication plan will cover the period from June 1 until October 31, 2026. This is the peak visitor period for TIR with many nature-based activities such as whale watching and water-based tours being run during the winter and spring seasons. You have been commissioned by the management team at TIR to develop a Marketing Plan for the last six months of 2026. Your Marketing Plan will focus on eco-certified tourism experiences and events. There are 2 parts to your Marketing Plan. Part 1 (Assessment 1) is a Market Analysis, which is a critical foundation for a Marketing Plan. In Part 2 (Assessment 2) you use the information from your Market Analysis to develop your complete Marketing Plan. YOUR TASK You will be randomly allocated one of the tourism or event experiences (hereafter referred to as 'activity') at TIR. Prepare a 3500 words Market Analysis for your allocated activity using the following sections: Organisation Overview (approximately 500 words) · This is an overview of TIR, not your activity. · This overview could include the range of activities, experiences and the unique services that TIR provides; where it is located; how it is accessed etc. · Caution – be objective. Brochures, websites etc are often very subjective. PESTEL analysis (approximately 1,500 words) · Review TIR’s external and internal environments using a PESTEL analysis o ALL six (6) PESTEL elements must be included in your analysis. o Focus on one (1) positive and one (negative) for each of the PESTEL elements o Approximately 100 words for each sub-section. · Include a Report Card for TIR. (Format discussed in class) · Relate the PESTLE analysis to your allocated activity. Marketing Research and Customer Insights (approximately 700 words) This section incorporates the first four phases in the research process (identify the marketing problem, investigate the available resources, research plan; data analysis and interpretation). · Identify and describe the current market segments that attend your activity · Identify a marketing opportunity for your activity · Justify this opportunity using a range of relevant resources. These could include industry research; government documents (statistics); websites; academic papers; industry reports; news releases etc · Devise a research plan to gather primary data to explore the viability of the opportunity you identified. o Important : you will not collect primary data. Instead, list and justify a maximum of 10 questions (qualitative or quantitative) that you would ask potential visitors and/or stakeholders related to your activity. · Explain how you would analyse and interpret the data if you were to collect the primary data Consider how your marketing opportunity matches the existing marketing approach (segmentation, positioning and branding) of TIR. Recommendation ( approximately 800 words) · Provide ONE recommendation that relates to eco-certification of your activity and its marketing potential for TIR. · There can be sub sections to your recommendation. · Your recommendation can be supported by literature and/or industry practice. · Your recommendation could relate to what TIR could do or what TIR could encourage tourists to do once they leave TIR. References · 5-10 references · APA (7th edition) · References are NOT included in the word count. For guidance refer to UQ library website. Visual aids and Appendices No more than 5 visual aids (e.g. photographs, tables) can be included in your market analysis. Extra tables, statistics etc should be in the appendices. o No more than 5 visual aids (e.g. photographs, tables) can be included in your market analysis. o Extra tables, statistics etc should be in the appendices. AI Statement Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description

assessment_details.title: Marketing Plan
assessment_details.other_conditions: Peer assessed. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: TRVT2001: Tourism & Event Marketing Type: Marketing Plan Assessment Percentage: 35% Word Limit : Infographic (6 pages) + poster Learning Objectives Assessed: 2, 3, 4, 5 (Refer to Course ECP) Due Date : Thursday the 21st of May at 1pm Assignment Type: Group (of 4) THE SCENARIO In Assessment 2 you will build on the Market Analysis of your activity at Tangalooma Island Resort (TIR) that you developed in Assessment 1. You will work in a team to develop a Marketing Plan for (TIR). Note: you can use the feedback from Assessment 1 to help guide you with Assessment 2. Your team comprises 4 students who were allocated the same activity as you for Assessment 1. Teams will be finalised in class in Week 7. There are 2 parts to this Assessment task: A. Team Task B. Individual Task TEAM TASK Collectively consider the information and feedback from all 4 team members Market Analysis (Assessment 1) tasks to develop a Marketing Plan for your activity. Your Marketing Plan will be presented as infographic pages plus a poster. There are 2 parts to your team task: Task 1 - infographic Task 2 - poster Note: these are to be submitted as one document. Instructions will be discussed in class and made available on Blackboard. Task 1 - infographic In your group design an infographic (approximately 6 pages) that covers the key points relating to your Marketing Plan. Ensure your infographic incorporates informative text and engaging images or graphics that effectively explain the communication design strategies to the management team at TIR. The following pages must be included in your Marketing Plan: Organisation overview (1 infographic page) · Summary of TIR as it is now · You can use relevant information from Assessment 1 (Market Analysis) Market segmentation, targeting, positioning and branding (1 infographic page) · Identify 2 target markets for your Marketing Plan · Apply the 4 bases of segmentation, as discussed in class. Product portfolio and innovation + evaluation (1 infographic page) · Design 2 SMART objectives for your activity · Justify why these product development innovations would be considered by your target markets Integrated marketing communications (plan and focus) (2 infographic pages) · Design 3 IMC objectives and justify why they are appropriate for promoting your product innovation. (half an infographic page) · Choose 3 tactics that support your IMC objectives. One of your tactics is your co-pilot poster (see Task 2). (half an infographic page per tactic) · Explain how the 3 IMC tactics can be synergised. · Be specific – ‘social media’ or ‘advertising’ is too general. ‘Instagram’ or ‘newspaper advertising’ is specific. · You can use tactics that TIR is currently using, however you need to make some changes to align with your plan. · You are not required to provide a budget for the Marketing Plan but you should consider TIR's size and whether it could realistically afford your tactics References (separate infographic page) · APA (7th edition) · A minimum of 5 references is required. Note: There are several different types of infographics that can be used including Canva, Piktochart and Venngage. Options can be found here: https://my.uq.edu.au/information-and-services/information-technology/software/software-catalogue . Canva is a great option as this is free-of-charge for UQ students. Task 2 - poster As a team use an AI generator to design a one page poster for TIR based on your activity. It is expected that you design a virtual influencer to be the symbol or spokesperson for your campaign. This influencer can be a person, an animal or an object (e.g. ship). There should also be pictures in the poster. Note: AI generator - Copilot is an AI generator that is free for UQ students. Step 1: Research TIR and your activity Consider: What makes TIR unique? What activities, attractions, and visual elements could you include? How can you appeal to your target market while staying authentic to the destination? Step 2: Co-write Your Prompt with your AI generator Work with your AI generator as a creative partner. Make these key decisions: Character: Person, animal, anthropomorphic? Realistic or stylised? What personality? Art Style: Photorealism, cartoon, vintage poster, Pixar 3D, pop art, something else? Composition: What TIR elements appear? (dolphins, shipwrecks, sand dunes, activities) What's the mood? Tip: Ask your AI generator questions like "What art styles work well for tourism posters aimed at (for example 20-30 year olds/Chinese families)?" Everything needs to have a rationale - be ready to explain your choices. Step 3: Generate Your Image Use your AI generator to create your poster. Expect iteration! Your first image won't be perfect. Refine your prompt, add details, try different styles. Keep track of what works and what doesn't. Step 4: Overview (500 words) Explain why this virtual influencer will work. Also answer these questions: Process: What worked collaborating with AI? What was frustrating? How many iterations? Outputs: What worked well? What AI "slop" appeared? (weird fingers, blurred text, odd details) Artificial Intelligence (AI) in Tourism and Events: How useful was AI? Where was human creativity essential? How might AI be received in tourism? What are the ethical considerations? INDIVIDUAL TASK Question and Answer (Q&A) session As a final session you will be meeting individually with your Course Co-ordinator or Facilitator for a Q&A session early in week 13, with a second chance late in week 13. Dates and times TBA. In this Q&A session you could be asked to answer questions about your Marketing Report from the perspective of some or all of the following: executive manager; market analyst; communications designer; administration This is a pass/fail requirement of your assessment. Fail results in a mark of 0/35 for this assessment task. Note: all Q&A sessions will be recorded as per UQ policy. Further guidelines for this assessment task as well as a marking rubric will be discussed in class and made available on the course Blackboard site. AI Statement: This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools. Read full task description Hide full task description

More detail will be provided in class and on Blackboard. AI Statement This assessment task is to be completed in-person. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description

learning_resources.library_resources: Library resources are available on the UQ Library website .

learning_activities.topic: Course overview and marketing process This week students will be introduced to tourism and events marketing. An overview of the marketing process used by tourism and event organisations is also provided. This week also provides a critical analysis of tourism and events as experiences. Learning outcomes: L01, L02

Destination marketing This week students will define what a destination is and how it is to be marketed. There is also a discussion on the different marketing orientations that DMOs may consider in tourism and events. Students will learn to develop a portfolio of tourism attractions and events. There is also an overview of how some events can be successful whereas others may fail. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03

Marketing environments This week students will learn to explain marketing’s role in strategic planning. They will also gain knowledge about the elements of a marketing plan. Students will also learn to describe the environmental forces that affect tourism and event organisations’ ability to create valued offerings. Finally, a discussion is provided on how tourism and events businesses can react to the marketing environment. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03

Marketing research and customer insights This week students will learn the role of marketing research in tourism and event marketing. Here, they will learn the steps involved in the research process. Students will also learn to identify and use a range of data sources. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03

Marketing planning Students will review key marketing concepts including segmentation and targeting and how they relate to tourism and events. In addition, this week students will review and apply the principles of services marketing in creating strategies for event and tourism products. Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L05

Tourism and events marketing mix- product strategy This week students will discuss tourism and events experiences and learn about the destination area lifecycle. A focus on product development is also provided. Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L05

Tourism and events marketing mix- communication strategy (#1) This week students will learn the first part of a communication strategy. Here, they will learn about positioning, branding and the communication process employed in tourism and events. The message and media strategy is also learned and the concept of integrated marketing communications is presented. Learning outcomes: L02, L04, L05

Tourism and events marketing mix- communication strategy (#2) This week students will learn about the media strategy and its importance to tourism and events. They will also learn to describe and evaluate the major different elements of IMC and how they relate to tourism and events. Learning outcomes: L02, L04, L05

Communications and technology This week students will learn about technological innovations relating to digital marketing that have been employed in industry. Here, students will learn about social media platforms such as Instagram and TikTok. Learning outcomes: L02, L04, L05

Labor Day (Public Holiday) Check Blackboard for resources to review on designing infographics. This will be further discussed in class. Labour Day Public Holiday - Monday 4th May 2026 - Check Blackboard for announcements about affected classes

Virtual Influencer Marketing and Artificial Intelligence This week introduces students to the important role of virtual influencer marketing and artificial intelligence in tourism and events. Learning outcomes: L02, L04, L05

Tourism and events marketing mix- pricing strategy This week students will learn about revenue sources that are applied by tourism and event organisations. Students will also learn about pricing objectives and strategies. An overview of ticketing systems is also provided. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04

Tourism and events marketing mix- place and distribution strategy This week students will learn about the importance of distribution for tourism and event organisations. There is also an opportunity for consultation for the final examination. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04

policies_and_procedures: University policies and procedures apply to all aspects of student life. As a UQ student, you must comply with University-wide and program-specific requirements, including the: Student Code of Conduct Policy Student Integrity and Misconduct Policy and Procedure Assessment Procedure Examinations Procedure Reasonable Adjustments for Students Policy and Procedure AI for Assessment Guide Learn more about UQ policies on my.UQ and the Policy and Procedure Library .
